For a first order reaction, a plot of ln k (y-axis) and $\frac{1}{T}$ (x-axis) gave the straight line with slope equal to $-10^3 k$ and intercept equal to 2.303 (on y-axis). What is the activation energy ($E_a$ in kJ mol$^{-1}$) of the reaction? (Given R = 8.314 J mol$^{-1}$K$^{-1}$)

(ii) ammonium dichromate

questions.text:

(i) Ti (IV) is more stable than Ti(III) and Ti(II)
(ii) Among 3d – series elements (from Z = 22 to 29) only copper has positive reduction potential ($M^{2+}/M$)

The molecular formula of a coordinate complex is $\text{CoH}_{12}\text{O}_6\text{Cl}_3$. When one mole of this aqueous solution of complex is reacted with excess of aqueous AgNO₃ solution, three moles of AgCl was formed. What is the correct formula of the complex?
Match the following. The correct answer is

options.text: A – II; B – III; C – I; D – IV

A – III; B – IV; C – I; D – II
A – III; B – I; C – IV; D – II

options.text: It is a broad spectrum antibiotic
It is used to treat typhoid

questions.text: A halogen compound X ($\text{C}_4\text{H}_9\text{Br}$) on hydrolysis gave alcohol Y. The alcohol Y undergoes dehydration with 20% $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$ at 358 K. What is X?

An alcohol X ($\text{C}_5\text{H}_{12}\text{O}$) when reacted with conc. HCl and anhydrous $\text{ZnCl}_2$ produces turbidity instantly. The alcohol X can be prepared from which of the following reactions?

options.text: Reaction of isopropyl magnesium bromide with ethanal
Reaction of ethyl magnesium bromide with propanal

questions.text:
Assertion (A): Chlorobenzene is not formed in the reaction of phenol with thionyl chloride
Reason (R): In phenol, carbon – oxygen bond has partial double bond character
The correct answer is

options.text: (A) and (R) are correct. (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
(A) and (R) are correct, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
